title: find_copa_do_brasil_final matches Libertadores too
evidence: query_engine.py:140 filters competition with str.contains("copa"), which matches both "Copa do Brasil" and "Copa Libertadores", so cup finals mix competitions
suggestion: Filter competition == "Copa do Brasil" exactly instead of substring "copa"

title: Standings double-count overlapping Brasileirao datasets
evidence: data_loader.py:123,207 both label rows "Brasileirao Serie A" (Brasileirao_Matches.csv + novo_campeonato 2003-2019 + extended BR-Football), and get_standings (query_engine.py:490) aggregates by that label, so a season covered by multiple files inflates played/points beyond 38 rounds
suggestion: Deduplicate matches or track source dataset so a single season maps to one canonical file before computing standings

title: Brazilian-club detection uses brittle substring regex
evidence: query_engine.py:420-429 matches clubs via hardcoded substrings like "atletico"/"sport" which also match non-Brazilian clubs (e.g. Atletico Madrid); a Brazilian national at such a club is counted as being at a Brazilian club
suggestion: Match against a curated Brazilian-club list (BRAZILIAN_CLUBS already exists in data_loader) rather than loose substrings

title: BRAZILIAN_CLUBS constant is dead code
evidence: data_loader.py:28 defines BRAZILIAN_CLUBS but it is never referenced anywhere in the package (grep shows only the definition)
suggestion: Use it for Brazilian-club filtering (see F3) or remove it

title: Aggregations iterate rows in Python rather than vectorized pandas
evidence: query_engine.py:186,500,691 use df.iterrows() loops for stats/standings/away-record; correct but slower than groupby/vectorized ops
suggestion: Vectorize hot aggregations; current perf still passes the <2s/<5s test gates (tests lines 933-951)
